mechanism_narrative: Positive monthly momentum in retail sales indicates resilient household demand and near-term revenue growth for listed firms, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days when RSAFS growth is stronger than its recent trend.
evidence_refs: fact:latest snapshot RSAFS value 507533.0 as of t-1mo
failure_condition: Reject if higher trailing 3-month RSAFS growth is followed by positive 20-day S&P 500 returns no more often than baseline, or if walk-forward Rank IC is below 0.03 over two consecutive 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: A federal funds rate crossing above its trailing 12-month high tightens discount rates and raises financing costs, reducing equity valuation multiples and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS crosses above its trailing 12-month high
evidence_refs: fact:latest snapshot FEDFUNDS value 1.91 as of t-1mo
failure_condition: Reject if triggered observations produce positive 20-day S&P 500 returns in more than 60% of walk-forward cases or if average triggered returns are not below unconditional returns.

mechanism_narrative: Payroll gains combined with a falling unemployment rate identify broad labor-income growth without visible labor-market stress, supporting consumption and earnings and producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:latest snapshot UNRATE value 3.9 as of t-1mo
failure_condition: Reject if the joint PAYEMS-up and UNRATE-down signal has a 60-day forward win rate below 50% and its mean return is not higher than periods without the joint signal.

mechanism_narrative: A material decline in industrial production signals weakening real activity and margin pressure in cyclical sectors, causing investors to mark down earnings expectations and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO falls by more than 0.5% over 3 months
evidence_refs: fact:latest snapshot INDPRO value 107.9989 as of t-1mo
failure_condition: Reject if INDPRO decline triggers are followed by positive 20-day S&P 500 returns in more than 60% of observations or if triggered mean returns exceed unconditional mean returns.

mechanism_narrative: A strong rebound in housing starts raises expectations for construction activity, durable-goods demand, and credit creation, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days after HOUST momentum turns sharply upward.
candidate_trigger: HOUST rises by more than 10% over 6 months
evidence_refs: fact:latest snapshot HOUST value 1173.0 as of t-2mo
failure_condition: Reject if HOUST rebound triggers do not deliver higher 60-day S&P 500 returns than non-trigger periods, or if the triggered 60-day win rate is below 50% in walk-forward tests.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating CPI while the federal funds rate is rising indicates inflation pressure that forces tighter policy, lifting discount rates faster than nominal earnings expectations and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL 3-month growth accelerates and FEDFUNDS 3-month change > 0
evidence_refs: fact:latest snapshot CPIAUCSL value 251.286 as of t-1mo
failure_condition: Reject if the joint inflation-acceleration and rate-hike signal is followed by positive 20-day S&P 500 returns in more than 60% of cases or has nonnegative average excess return versus baseline.

mechanism_narrative: A very low unemployment rate combined with slowing payroll growth identifies late-cycle labor tightness, where wage pressure and fading job creation compress margins and produce negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE < 4.0 and PAYEMS 3-month growth is below its trailing 12-month average
evidence_refs: fact:latest snapshot UNRATE value 3.9 as of t-1mo
failure_condition: Reject if triggered late-cycle labor-tightness observations have 60-day forward returns equal to or above unconditional returns, or if the signal's Rank IC is below 0.03 over any 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly CPI increase crossing above 0.4% signals an inflation shock that raises the probability of tighter monetary policy and immediate valuation pressure, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:latest snapshot CPIAUCSL value 251.286 as of t-1mo
failure_condition: Reject if CPIAUCSL monthly growth crossings above 0.4% are followed by nonnegative average 5-day S&P 500 returns or if the triggered negative-return hit rate is below 50%.
